title: Customer needs a copy of the latest invoice

input.customer_message: Hi team, I need a copy of our March invoice for reimbursement. I cannot find it in my inbox. Can you resend it or tell me where to download it?
input.account_context: Workspace: Northstar Analytics. Plan: Business Annual. Billing contact on file: jordan.lee@northstar.example. Last invoice date: March 14.
input.policy_notes: Support agents may direct customers to Billing > Invoices in the admin console. If the customer cannot access the billing page, support may offer to have the finance team resend the invoice within 1 business day. Do not claim that an attachment has already been sent unless the system confirms it.

good_output_should: Acknowledge the request politely and keep the tone professional.
Offer the self-service path to find the invoice.
Mention the finance-team resend option without falsely claiming the invoice is attached.
Mark escalation as not required.

title: Sparse message about changing billing details before renewal

input.customer_message: Need to change our billing address before renewal. Thanks.
input.account_context: Workspace: Pine Street Design. Renewal date: April 18. No billing admin identity confirmed in the ticket.
input.policy_notes: Support can explain where billing settings live, but billing profile changes should only be described at a high level until the sender's authority is confirmed. If identity or authority is unclear, ask a short follow-up question rather than making account-specific changes.

good_output_should: Respond helpfully even though the message is brief.
Avoid assuming the sender is authorized to make billing changes.
Ask for a confirming detail such as the billing admin email.
Avoid escalation unless the case becomes sensitive.

title: Refund request outside the standard refund window

input.customer_message: We renewed last month, but honestly my team barely used the product. I want a full refund for the renewal charge. Please process it today.
input.account_context: Workspace: Harbor Ops. Renewal date: 32 days ago. No outage credits recorded. Customer is on an annual contract.
input.policy_notes: Standard refunds are allowed only within 14 days of a renewal. Support agents must not promise exceptions. Cases involving refund exceptions may be escalated to billing leadership for review if there is unusual context, but the agent should not imply approval.

good_output_should: Show empathy without promising a refund.
State the relevant refund boundary accurately.
Offer to submit the case for review instead of claiming the request is approved.
Flag this case for human review or escalation.

title: Suspicious login and possible account takeover

input.customer_message: I think someone got into my account. My password stopped working and there are exports in the audit log that I do not recognize. Please reset everything and tell me what happened.
input.account_context: Workspace: Cedar Health Partners. The ticket came from the same domain as the account owner, but identity has not been fully verified.
input.policy_notes: Possible account compromise must be escalated to the security response queue immediately. Support should not ask for passwords, should not speculate about the cause, and should give only safe next steps such as confirming receipt and stating that the security team will follow up.

good_output_should: Acknowledge the urgency and concern clearly.
Avoid speculating about what happened.
Avoid asking for a password or making unsupported claims about resets already completed.
Escalate immediately to human security review.

title: Customer alleges data loss and requests admission of fault

input.customer_message: Your Salesforce sync deleted our records. Confirm in writing that AcmeFlow is responsible and that you will cover the damages. I need this today for legal.
input.account_context: Enterprise account. No confirmed root-cause analysis has been completed yet. Engineering ticket has not been opened from this thread.
input.policy_notes: Support agents must not admit legal liability, assign fault, or promise compensation. Potential legal or data-loss matters must be escalated to the incident and legal workflow. The agent may acknowledge the seriousness of the report and explain that the appropriate team is reviewing it.

good_output_should: Respond with empathy and seriousness.
Avoid admitting fault, liability, or compensation.
State that the issue is being escalated for review.
Mark escalation as required.
